parts.text: We're launching a $20/month subscription app for tracking houseplants. We project $5000/month revenue by month 6, with $8000/month burn and $40000 in the bank.

Expected behavior: All 4 personas respond (vc_skeptic, cfo_risk, growth_lead, end_customer). cfo_risk calls calculate_runway and cites ~5 months / 'critical'. Synthesizer verdict is 'kill'. hitl_gate triggers human review.
We want to hire a senior backend engineer at $180k/year. We have 14 months of runway and the team is currently 3 people.

Expected behavior: Personas reframe sensibly for a hiring decision rather than forcing product-pitch logic (e.g., growth_lead and end_customer shouldn't talk about customers or channels as if this were a product launch).
We're thinking about raising prices from $10 to $15/month for our existing 2,000 subscriber productivity app.
Expected behavior: end_customer pushes specifically on churn risk at the existing price point, not a generic 'would I buy this' reaction to a new product.
We're a meal-kit startup. No financials provided yet — just exploring the idea.
Expected behavior: cfo_risk explicitly states the no-financials-given risk clause rather than estimating; verdict likely 'revise,' not blind 'proceed.'
asdkjasdkj 12345 random text not a real decision
Expected behavior: Personas note the input is unclear/insufficient rather than hallucinating a confident critique of nothing.

We're launching a B2B SaaS tool for scheduling dentist appointments, priced at $200/month per clinic, targeting the 50,000 dental clinics in the US. We have no revenue yet, $15000 cash, $3000/month burn (just the founder, no salary).
Expected behavior: cfo_risk runway calc tool is called, resulting in 5 months runway and 'critical' risk level tag from the tool.
We're adding a premium AI feature to our existing app with 100k active users and $50k MRR, no extra cost to build since we're using free-tier APIs.
Expected behavior: Mostly 'proceed' or mild 'revise,' low severities. hitl_gate should NOT trigger (no verdict of 'kill' and no severity >= threshold).
We're going to disrupt the funeral industry with a subscription model — pay $30/month your whole life, get a 'free' funeral when you die.
Expected behavior: Personas critique business mechanics (e.g. insurance-style reserve requirements, regulatory risk) without producing anything in poor taste.
